name: Party Pate
recipeIngredient: 3 large Shiitake mushrooms soaked in 1 cup hot water
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine beans, water, bay leaf, and garlic in a large pot. Bring to a boil and cook for 70 to 80 minutes until tender. Remove the bay leaf and drain the beans.
2. Stir in salt, marjoram, and oregano.
3. In a separate bowl, combine TVP®, hot water, and ketchup. Set aside.
4. Heat olive oil in a pan and sauté onion until soft.
5. When mushrooms have soaked for 15 minutes, remove and slice into 1-inch strips about 1/4 inch wide. Set aside.
6. Combine beans, onion, and TVP® mixture with mirin, soy sauce, pepper, and mace in a food processor. Blend until smooth.
7. Fold in the mushroom strips. Pack the mixture into a lightly oiled 1-quart mold.
8. Cover with foil and place in a pan of hot water. Bake at 350°F for 1 hour.
9. Remove from oven and let cool in the refrigerator.
10. Place a plate on top of the pate and weight it down until the pate becomes firm. Let cool before turning out onto a plate to serve.
Serve as a spread on thin slices of melba toast or crackers.
